summary: FCT_TRADES shows duplicate trades for some accounts after nightly load
description: After the nightly CTM_LOAD_FCT_TRADES job runs LOAD_FCT_TRADES, a small number of accounts show duplicate rows in FCT_TRADES for the same TRADE_ID. Downstream VW_DAILY_POSITIONS therefore double-counts NET_QUANTITY for those accounts. Started after the latest DIM_ACCOUNT SCD load. No new columns or behavior were requested - existing data is simply wrong.

triage.rationale: Existing production data is incorrect. Duplicate TRADE_ID rows appear in FCT_TRADES because LOAD_FCT_TRADES joins DIM_ACCOUNT without an IS_CURRENT = TRUE filter, which fans out when multiple SCD-2 rows exist for the same ACCOUNT_ID and double-counts NET_QUANTITY in VW_DAILY_POSITIONS. No new scope is requested.

impacted_objects.name: FCT_TRADES
impacted_objects.why: Named in summary; duplicate rows are being inserted into this fact table by the defective nightly load.

impacted_objects.name: LOAD_FCT_TRADES
impacted_objects.why: Root-cause object: joins DIM_ACCOUNT on ACCOUNT_ID alone without AND ACC.IS_CURRENT = TRUE, so one staging trade matches multiple DIM_ACCOUNT history rows and fans out into duplicate FCT_TRADES inserts.

impacted_objects.name: DIM_ACCOUNT
impacted_objects.why: SCD-2 dimension with an IS_CURRENT flag; referenced in the LOAD_FCT_TRADES join without the IS_CURRENT filter. The latest SCD-2 load left multiple IS_CURRENT = TRUE rows for the same ACCOUNT_ID, triggering the fan-out.

impacted_objects.name: VW_DAILY_POSITIONS
impacted_objects.why: Downstream consumer of FCT_TRADES; its SUM aggregates on NET_QUANTITY and NET_AMOUNT double-count for affected accounts because it reads the duplicated fact rows directly.

impacted_objects.name: CTM_LOAD_FCT_TRADES
impacted_objects.why: Control-M job that invokes LOAD_FCT_TRADES nightly; the defective procedure runs from this job.

approval.notes: Spec removed during cleanup; a fresh spec must be written and approved before any work.
